reinvestment_behavior.draft: Moderna is reinvesting heavily in R&D and commercial infrastructure despite negative operating income; the Senior should judge whether that spending is disciplined option creation or value-destructive burn.
reinvestment_behavior.source_label: MRNA FY2025 Form 10-K R&D and operating-expense discussion
reinvestment_behavior.excerpt_or_summary: The fixture links high R&D intensity and operating losses to the filed spine and the pipeline-development strategy.
reinvestment_behavior.filing_reference: MRNA FY2025 Form 10-K, MD&A and EDGAR-derived spine inputs

shareholder_return_behavior.draft: Shareholder returns are not the central capital-allocation test for this route; dilution, buybacks, and financing flexibility must be judged against the cash runway and pipeline funding need.
shareholder_return_behavior.source_label: MRNA FY2025 Form 10-K equity and liquidity discussion
shareholder_return_behavior.excerpt_or_summary: The filing and EDGAR share-count facts provide the share-base context for a development-stage optionality case.
shareholder_return_behavior.filing_reference: MRNA FY2025 Form 10-K, liquidity and stockholders' equity disclosures

balance_sheet_discipline.draft: Balance-sheet discipline hinges on preserving cash and marketable resources long enough to fund clinical readouts and launches without forcing punitive financing.
balance_sheet_discipline.source_label: MRNA FY2025 EDGAR-derived cash and liability facts
balance_sheet_discipline.excerpt_or_summary: EDGAR-derived cash, liabilities, and lease-debt concepts frame runway and financing risk.
